report_name: Vect Ransomware Forges Alliance with BreachForums and TeamPCP to Industrialize Attacks

threat_actors.name: Vect
threat_actors.description: A ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) group that emerged in late 2025. It has formed a strategic alliance with BreachForums and TeamPCP to industrialize ransomware deployment. The group uses a custom C++ locker, TOR-only infrastructure, and the TOX protocol for affiliate communication.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through industrialized ransomware deployment and double-extortion.

threat_actors.name: BreachForums
threat_actors.description: A notorious cybercrime marketplace that acts as a recruitment and logistics hub for the Vect RaaS operation. Vect distributes affiliate keys to BreachForums members to crowdsource its attack force.
threat_actors.goals: Facilitating cybercrime, including affiliate recruitment for ransomware operations.

threat_actors.name: TeamPCP
threat_actors.description: A hacking group specializing in supply chain attacks to harvest credentials and access tokens. It funnels these compromised credentials to Vect affiliates as part of a strategic alliance.
threat_actors.goals: Large-scale credential harvesting through supply chain attacks.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Vect Ransomware payload is deployed across victim networks to encrypt critical files and servers as the primary impact of the attack.

attack_patterns.name: Financial Theft
attack_patterns.description: The core motivation of the Vect RaaS operation is financial extortion, demanding payment for file decryption and to prevent data leakage.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The primary initial access vector for Vect affiliates is the use of compromised credentials and access tokens stolen by the TeamPCP group.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: Attackers exfiltrate sensitive data to Vect's servers before encryption to be used in a double-extortion scheme.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
attack_patterns.description: TeamPCP conducts campaigns targeting vulnerabilities in open-source tools like LiteLLM and Trivy to steal credentials and access tokens.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware behavior includes deleting Volume Shadow Copies using tools like vssadmin to prevent easy system recovery.

malware.name: Vect Ransomware
malware.description: A custom C++-based ransomware locker used by the Vect RaaS group. It is deployed by affiliates to encrypt victim systems.

tools.name: LiteLLM
tools.description: An open-source development tool targeted by TeamPCP in supply chain attacks to harvest credentials.

tools.name: Trivy
tools.description: An open-source security tool targeted by TeamPCP in supply chain attacks to harvest credentials.

tools.name: Tor
tools.description: Used by the Vect group for its command and control and data exfiltration infrastructure to enhance anonymity.

tools.name: TOX
tools.description: The TOX protocol is used by the Vect RaaS for affiliate communication. The presence of a client like 'tox-core' is considered suspicious.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: A legitimate Windows tool abused by ransomware to delete volume shadow copies, inhibiting system recovery.

vulnerabilities.name: LiteLLM Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: An unspecified vulnerability in the open-source tool LiteLLM was targeted by TeamPCP to steal credentials and access tokens.

vulnerabilities.name: Trivy Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: An unspecified vulnerability in the open-source tool Trivy was targeted by TeamPCP to steal credentials and access tokens.

identities.name: Guesty
identities.description: A technology company confirmed as a victim of the Vect ransomware alliance, listed on the group's leak site.

identities.name: USHA International Limited
identities.description: A manufacturing company confirmed as a victim of the Vect ransomware alliance, listed on the group's leak site.

identities.name: S&P Global
identities.description: A financial services company mentioned as a potential victim of the Vect ransomware alliance.

infrastructure.name: Vect C2 Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: The command and control and data exfiltration infrastructure used by the Vect RaaS, which is described as being TOR-only.

indicators.name: TOX Client Process Execution
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: TOX Client Process Execution
id: 2b1a9c8d-e3f4-4a5b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of 'tox-core', a client for the TOX protocol, which is used by Vect ransomware affiliates for communication.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/news/vect-formalizes-breachforums-and-teampcp-alliance-to-push-model-for-industrialized-ransomware-scale-raas-operations/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-21
tags:
  - attack.t1071
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\tox-core.exe'
    OriginalFileName:
      - 'tox-core.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the 'tox-core' process, a client for the TOX protocol used by Vect affiliates for communication.

indicators.name: Vect Ransomware File Extension
indicators.pattern:
title: Vect Ransomware File Extension Detected
id: 9f8e7d6c-5b4a-4938-2716-0987654321ab
status: experimental
description: 'Detects files being created or renamed with the .vect extension, which is associated with Vect Ransomware.'
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/news/vect-formalizes-breachforums-and-teampcp-alliance-to-push-model-for-industrialized-ransomware-scale-raas-operations/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-21
tags:
  - attack.t1486
level: critical
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.vect'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of files with the '.vect' extension, indicating Vect Ransomware activity.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 8e7d6c5b-4a39-3827-1609-87654321abcf
status: experimental
description: 'Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware tactic to inhibit system recovery.'
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/news/vect-formalizes-breachforums-and-teampcp-alliance-to-push-model-for-industrialized-ransomware-scale-raas-operations/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-21
tags:
  - attack.t1490
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'Delete'
      - 'Shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of vssadmin to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware technique to inhibit system recovery.

relationships.target_name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
